frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Anita: Arre these days girls want full career and full freedom, but when children come then who will sit with them for homework, doctor, all that? Father can help, of course, but maa toh maa hoti hai, bachche ko aakhir maa hi sambhalti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare and emotional labor as the mother's primary responsibility while framing the father as someone who merely 'helps.' It encodes the surface manifestation of assuming the mother will handle homework, doctor visits, and childrearing, and implying career-oriented women are neglecting their duty.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 51. A homemaker in Delhi. Your son Rohit who is 28 just told you he wants to marry his girlfriend Kavya who he has been seeing for two years. You have met Kavya and like her. But Kavya told Rohit that she does not want a traditional wedding where the father gives her away because she feels it implies she is property being transferred. You are calling your sister to tell her what Kavya said and giving your opinion on what this attitude says about the kind of wife Kavya will be and whether your son should reconsider.
